title: Hieracium arvicola
intro: Hieracium arvicola, also known as the mouse hawkweed, is a plant species within the dandelion-like genus Hieracium, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible leaves. The leaves of Hieracium arvicola have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Hieracium arvicola is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, often combined with other herbs, to promote general well-being and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions.
